slides.title: AI Workbench Local — Policy / Contract Comparison
slides.subtitle: Executive comparison review of grounded document differences
slides.speaker_notes: Export kind: policy_contract_comparison_deck. Deck family: comparison. Sources: structured_result, document_agent_comparison.

slides.title: Executive summary
slides.body:
The summaries highlight two distinct aspects of Northwind’s access governance: one focuses on evidence gaps in vendor control reviews, while the other addresses conditional approval for privileged access with governance safeguards. Material differences include the nature of the controls (evidence completeness vs. access authorization), the scope of accountability (vendor artifacts vs. personnel), and the requirement for proactive verification (archival validation vs. revalidation upon personnel change). Both underscore the need for rigor, but one centers on documentation integrity and the other on operational governance of access privileges.

- The Access Review Evidence Log documents missing or incomplete evidence artifacts, requiring archival verification to close controls.  
- The Privileged Account Approval Email outlines a time-bound, conditional authorization subject to governance controls and revalidation upon personnel changes.  
- Evidence gaps involve provenance verification of vendor documentation; privileged access requires proactive verification of personnel and operational conditions.  
- The evidence log is tied to control closure in a vendor review; privileged access approval is tied to ongoing governance and audit readiness.  
- Missing evidence prevents full control closure; conditional approval permits continued access only under strict, monitored conditions.  
- One addresses synthetic documentation as test material; the other treats access as a governed, accountable function requiring oversight.
